message:
fix(database): MySQL row limit safety, Postgres orphaned txn cleanup, pool parking_lot

mysql.rs:
  When a result set exceeds max_rows, mark the connection as closed
  (self.inner.closed = true) before returning the error. The server
  is still streaming row packets that cannot be drained synchronously,
  so reusing the connection would cause protocol desync. Marking it
  closed forces the pool to discard it.

postgres.rs:
  Add clear_orphaned_transaction() calls at the top of query(),
  execute(), prepare(), execute_prepared(), fetch_prepared(), and
  close_prepared(). If a prior operation left the connection in an
  errored transaction state, this issues a ROLLBACK to restore it
  to a usable state before proceeding. Prevents "current transaction
  is aborted" cascading failures across pooled connection reuse.

pool.rs:
  Migrate from std::sync::Mutex to parking_lot::Mutex for the
  connection pool internals. Eliminates .unwrap() on lock() calls
  and avoids poison propagation, consistent with the rest of the
  codebase's parking_lot adoption.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
